title: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Shapes
description: Hardware profiles for OCI compute instances used by the SuperInstance/LAU stack

VM.Standard.E4.Flex.bestFor: General-purpose compute, Go services, Chapel compilation, API backends
VM.Standard.E4.Flex.notes: Flex shape allows granular OCPU/memory allocation. Best price-performance for x86 workloads.

VM.Standard3.Flex.notes: Use when Intel-specific instructions (AVX-512, VNNI) provide measurable benefit over AMD.

BM.Standard.E4.128.bestFor: High-performance bare-metal compute, large-scale simulation, batch processing
BM.Standard.E4.128.notes: Bare metal — no noisy neighbors. Ideal for benchmark baseline. Requires Oracle Cloud bare-metal provisioning.

A1.Flex.bestFor: Always-free ARM compute, edge gateway, CI/CD runners, cost-optimized workloads
A1.Flex.notes: The crown jewel of OCI free tier. 4 ARM OCPUs + 24GB RAM for free. Excellent for Go + C cross-compilation. Use Dockerfile.cpu for this target.

VM.GPU.A10.1.bestFor: GPU-accelerated inference, CUDA development, ML workloads
VM.GPU.A10.1.notes: Single A10 GPU — good middle ground between cost and GPU capability.

BM.GPU4.8.bestFor: Maximum GPU compute, large-scale training, massive parallel simulation
BM.GPU4.8.notes: 8x A100 (40GB each). Serious hardware for serious workloads.

regions.freeTierAvailable: All regions support A1.Flex free tier, subject to capacity
